danger_signals.visual_form: Zombie pirate appearance, undead visual deterioration, dark fortress imagery
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; resurrection as zombie indicates escalated danger vs. first game

danger_signals.visual_form: Imposing pirate character with authority markers
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate local threat; embargo enforcer preventing escape from Scabb Island

danger_signals.visual_form: Craftable doll object, visual representation of vulnerability
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural danger present; world-state includes magical threat vectors

danger_signals.visual_form: Elaborate mechanical and environmental hazards in LeChuck's fortress
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger; LeChuck's domain presents constructed threat scenarios

danger_signals.visual_form: Governor Phatt's prison, LeChuck's fortress cells, elaborate trap dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Political/power threat; world-state includes authoritarian containment mechanics

safety_signals.visual_form: International House of Mojo in swamp; mystical aesthetic with guidance function
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven for supernatural aid; refuge from immediate threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Character reunion sequences, interactive dialogue spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Emotional anchor point; relationship state reflects world stability despite conflict

safety_signals.visual_form: Captain Dread's vessel, functional transportation between islands
safety_signals.meaning: Mobility and escape routes available; world-state permits inter-island travel
Information safety; quest progression depends on gathering knowledge safely

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Zombie pirate iconography, fortress symbol, undead aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zombie transformation, undead minions, dark/corrupted environmental design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: LeChuck's island fortress; dark, gothic architecture; swamp associations with voodoo threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pirate-standard imagery, treasure maps, ship iconography
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Largo LaGrande as antagonist faction; mercenary hunters pursuing bounty
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scabb Island, Phatt Island, Booty Island; port towns; pirate gathering spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Governor Phatt's authority markers, imprisonment infrastructure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Phatt Island prison; government buildings; formal power structures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Voodoo doll, mystical symbols, swamp aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Voodoo magic as both aid and threat mechanic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: International House of Mojo, swamp environments, supernatural visual language

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Restricted movement between locations; visual barriers; island isolation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs unable to leave; Guybrush's escape attempts blocked; travel limited to chartered vessels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Undead aesthetic transformation; darker color palette; fortress becomes active threat location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased antagonist activity; pursuit mechanics activated; danger escalates across islands

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character interaction changes; dialogue reflects bitterness; emotional visual language shifts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elaine's goals diverge from Guybrush; eventual reunion sequence indicates state change possibility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map piece collection; inventory visual accumulation; journal/catalog updates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest markers active; NPC dialogue references map progress; environmental puzzles tied to map stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift to concrete bunker aesthetic; technological elements replace organic island visuals; lost parents area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New traversal mechanics; elevator systems; shift from outdoor to interior environmental challenges

environmental_storytelling.example: Scabb, Phatt, and Booty islands each house distinct plot elements and factions; traversal between them physically enacts quest progression
Governor Phatt's prison; LeChuck's fortress; International House of Mojo swamp location—each environment's visual design communicates faction authority and nature
Voodoo doll, map pieces, E ticket, first aid station—collectible items visually represent progress and world-state changes
Largo's authority markers; LeChuck's zombie transformation; Elaine's bitter demeanor—visual character design reflects current world conditions
Death-traps in LeChuck's fortress; maze-like jungle on Dinky Island; concrete bunker discovery—physical environments embody the growing existential threat
Mardi Gras party, spitting competition, bloodhound encounters, drinking contest—social and competitive activities tied to specific islands communicate local culture and quest necessity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active pursuit across islands; torture via voodoo mechanics; fortress occupation; escalating revenge arc
Embargo imposition; theft of Guybrush's possessions; use of LeChuck's beard to resurrect threat
Provides sanctuary; offers quest guidance; crafts voodoo dolls; dispenses supernatural knowledge
Imprisonment; bounty collection motivation; represents governmental power faction
Initially bitter refusal of Guybrush; eventual investigation of explosion; suggests latent care despite rift
Map analysis; kidnapping by LeChuck; determines Big Whoop location; represents knowledge node in quest chain
Presence on Dinky Island; provides jungle navigation assistance; indicates recurring character significance

notes: Monkey Island 2 communicates world-state primarily through location-based faction visualization, character appearance transformation (especially LeChuck's zombie conversion), and environmental architecture reflecting power structures. The game uses a three-island structure to manifest political fragmentation and embargo mechanics visually. Voodoo systems create a supernatural danger layer distinct from pirate/governmental threats. The shift from surface islands to underground facility at game's climax represents a major world-state revelation (artificial origin of Big Whoop). Safety signals concentrate around knowledge-givers (Voodoo Lady, Wally) and transportation access (Captain Dread). Danger signals escalate through resurrection mechanics and trap environments. The game's visual language transitions from tropical pirate aesthetics to industrial/concrete bunker design, signaling a hidden world-state beneath surface narrative.
